chunks.text: Bestie intros and some early table-setting around Friedberg potentially running for governor in California.
The discussion turns to Anthropic's generational run, what is driving the company's momentum, and why the market is treating the model race as a genuine platform shift.
The group asks whether OpenAI is getting more focused or slipping into panic mode as competition sharpens across research, products, and talent retention.
They unpack AI valuation impacts, where moats might still exist, and which parts of the stack are getting disrupted fastest as model quality keeps moving.
Liquidity speaker announcements and the broader 100x AI moment come into focus as they compare investor appetite with the pace of real enterprise adoption.
Two landmark social media verdicts against Meta shift the conversation toward platform liability, moderation risk, and how legal pressure could change incentives.
Sacks and Friedberg joining PCAST closes the episode with a broader look at policy influence, credibility, and the government's evolving relationship with frontier AI.
